description_long: This is a bulky, retro-style cathode ray tube (CRT) television with a rectangular, silver-gray front panel and a large, deep black casing extending backward. The front features a wide, flat screen displaying static, bordered by a thick bezel, and a speaker grille at the bottom. A terracotta plant pot with a lush, trailing green vine sits on top of the television, with leaves cascading down the front and side. The television's back and sides are solid black, with no visible ports or buttons. The overall design is utilitarian, with a focus on function over aesthetics, typical of older television models. The plant adds a touch of domesticity and visual interest to the otherwise industrial object.
description: A large, retro CRT television with a silver-gray front, thick black casing, and a potted trailing plant on top. The screen displays static, and the plant's vines drape over the front and side.
description_view_0: Front view showing the static-filled screen, silver bezel, speaker grille, and the plant pot with vines cascading down the front.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth of the black casing and the plant's vines draping along the side.
description_view_2: Rear view showing the solid black back of the television and the plant pot resting on top, with minimal vine visibility.
description_view_3: Angled rear-side view displaying the plant pot and vines, as well as the television's deep, angular casing.
attribute_bezel_design: thick, silver-gray, plastic, rectangular with integrated speaker grille
